description: Fix several CLI output UX issues: status command enumerates 76 disabled tool names instead of summarizing, config show exposes internal config keys, audit command 401 error lacks actionable guidance, telemetry command has a dead link, and config set --help shows a wrong example tool name.

userStories.title: status: show disabled tool count instead of enumerating 76 names
userStories.description: When the Chrome extension is not connected, `opentabs status` prints a 'Disabled Browser Tools' section that lists all 76 disabled tool names one-per-line. This creates a wall of 76+ lines that buries the key message (extension not connected). Replace the enumeration with a summary line: the count and the reason they're disabled.
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs status` when extension is not connected shows a single summary line for disabled browser tools (e.g. '76 browser tools disabled — connect the Chrome extension to enable') rather than listing each name
The disabled tools section is only shown if disabledBrowserTools is non-empty (current behavior preserved)
`opentabs status --json` output is unchanged (still includes full disabledBrowserTools array for programmatic use)

userStories.title: telemetry: fix dead learn-more URL
userStories.description: The telemetry status command (and first-run telemetry notice in `opentabs start`) prints 'Learn more: https://docs.opentabs.dev/telemetry' which is a dead link (returns connection error). The correct URL is https://opentabs.dev/docs/reference/telemetry. Fix both occurrences.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/cli/src/commands/telemetry.ts: the 'Learn more' line uses URL https://opentabs.dev/docs/reference/telemetry
platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts: the 'Learn more' line in the telemetry notice uses URL https://opentabs.dev/docs/reference/telemetry
No other occurrences of docs.opentabs.dev remain in the CLI codebase
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/telemetry.ts (line 55)
- platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts (line containing 'docs.opentabs.dev/telemetry')

Context:
In telemetry.ts at line 55:
```typescript
console.log(pc.dim('  Learn more: https://docs.opentabs.dev/telemetry'));
```
Same string appears in start.ts in the `showTelemetryNoticeIfNeeded` function. Both need to change to:
```typescript
console.log(pc.dim('  Learn more: https://opentabs.dev/docs/reference/telemetry'));
```

Constraints:
- Only change the URL string, nothing else
- Check for any other occurrences of 'docs.opentabs.dev' in the CLI (grep the platform/cli/src/ directory)

userStories.title: config show: hide internal fields; config set --help: fix wrong tool name example
userStories.description: Two config display issues: (1) `opentabs config show` displays internal config fields like `telemetryNoticeShown`, `version`, `localPluginDirs`, and `updateCheckIntervalMinutes` that are meaningless to users. These should be hidden. (2) `opentabs config set --help` shows the example `tool-permission.browser.browser_screenshot ask` but the actual tool name is `browser_screenshot_tab` — users who copy this example set a permission for a non-existent tool.
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs config show` does not display `telemetryNoticeShown`, `version`, `localPluginDirs`, or `updateCheckIntervalMinutes` fields
`opentabs config show` does display `localPlugins`, `permissions`, `settings`, and `port` (if set) as before
The inline help comment at the 'Invalid key format' error for tool-permission also shows `browser_screenshot_tab` (not `browser_screenshot`)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts (normalizeConfigForDisplay function, addHelpText for config set, handleSetToolPermission inline comment)

Context:
(1) Internal fields hidden from display:
In normalizeConfigForDisplay (lines 61-74), the function passes through ALL non-canonical keys. The canonical sections are defined at line 54:
```typescript
const CANONICAL_CONFIG_SECTIONS = ['localPlugins', 'permissions', 'settings'] as const;
```
All other keys (including `telemetryNoticeShown`, `version`, `localPluginDirs`, `updateCheckIntervalMinutes`, `telemetry`) are shown verbatim via the loop at lines 64-67.

The fix: add a HIDDEN_DISPLAY_KEYS set and skip those in the loop:
```typescript
const HIDDEN_DISPLAY_KEYS = new Set([
  'version',             // internal migration counter
  'telemetryNoticeShown', // legacy first-run flag
  'telemetry',           // opt-out flag (surfaced via opentabs telemetry status)
  'localPluginDirs',     // legacy alias for localPlugins (already shown)
  'updateCheckIntervalMinutes', // internal scheduling config
]);
// In the loop at line 64:
if (!(CANONICAL_CONFIG_SECTIONS as readonly string[]).includes(key) && !HIDDEN_DISPLAY_KEYS.has(key)) {
  normalized[key] = config[key];
}
```

Note: `additionalAllowedDirectories` should NOT be hidden — if the user has added allowed directories, they should see them. If empty, it's currently displayed as `additionalAllowedDirectories []` — this is acceptable for now.

(2) Wrong tool name in help:
At line 800 in the addHelpText for `config set`:
```typescript
$ opentabs config set tool-permission.browser.browser_screenshot ask
```
Change `browser_screenshot` to `browser_screenshot_tab` (that's the actual tool name).

Also in handleSetToolPermission at line 256:
```typescript
console.error('For browser tools: tool-permission.browser.browser_screenshot');
```
Change `browser_screenshot` to `browser_screenshot_tab` here too.

Constraints:
- Do NOT hide `port` from display (even though it's non-canonical)
- Do NOT hide `additionalAllowedDirectories` — it IS user-visible
- Do NOT change the `--json` output path (which returns the raw normalized config including all fields)
- The normalizeConfigForDisplay function adds canonical sections even if absent (localPlugins defaults to []); that behavior must be preserved

userStories.title: audit: improve 401 error message to match status command quality
userStories.description: The `opentabs audit` command shows 'Authentication failed. Is the server secret correct? Check ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json' for a 401 response. The `opentabs status` command has a better message for the same situation that also suggests actionable remedies. Align the audit 401 message with the status command's guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: audit.ts 401 error includes the hint 'Try restarting the server or running: opentabs config rotate-secret --confirm' (matching status.ts guidance)
audit.ts 401 error still mentions auth.json for context
The status command's 401 message is not changed
